166	/-! ### Lax-monoidal global sections: the section multiplication
167	
168	The global-sections functor `Γ(X, -)` is only *lax* monoidal: a pair of global
169	sections does not commute with sheafification, so the multiplication is a map,
170	not an isomorphism.  It is nonetheless `Γ(X, 𝒪_X)`-linear and is the data the
171	section graded ring is built from. -/
172	
173	/-- The **section multiplication** (`def:sectionMul`), the `Γ(X,𝒪_X)`-bilinear map
174	`Γ(X,F) ⊗_{Γ(X,𝒪_X)} Γ(X,G) → Γ(X, F ⊗ G)`.
175	
176	Its domain `(F.toPresheaf ⊗ G.toPresheaf)(X)` is, by the objectwise formula of
177	`PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`, the `Γ(X,𝒪_X)`-module
178	`Γ(X,F) ⊗_{Γ(X,𝒪_X)} Γ(X,G)` of elementary tensors of global sections; a pair
179	`(σ, τ)` is sent to `σ ⊗ τ`.  Postcomposing with the global-sections component of
180	the sheafification unit `η : P → P^#` (`def:sheafTensorObj`) lands in
181	`Γ(X, F ⊗ G)`.  As a morphism in `ModuleCat (Γ(X,𝒪_X))` it is automatically
182	`Γ(X,𝒪_X)`-bilinear; this records that linearity.  Axiom-clean: it is pure
183	sheafification-unit naturality, requiring no monoidal structure on `X.Modules`. -/
184	noncomputable def sectionsMul (F G : X.Modules) :
185	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
186	        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)).obj (Opposite.op ⊤) ⟶
187	      (tensorObj F G).val.obj (Opposite.op ⊤) :=
188	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app
189	      (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
190	        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G))).app (Opposite.op ⊤)
191	
192	/-! ### The strong-monoidality comparison `isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`
193	
194	Following `analogies/snap-route.md` (Analogue 1) and the blueprint proof of
195	`lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`: module sheafification is the
196	localization functor at the class `W := J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf R₀)` of
197	morphisms of presheaves of modules whose underlying abelian-presheaf morphism is a
198	local isomorphism (a `J.W` for the opens topology `J` on `X`). -/
199	
200	/-- The Grothendieck topology on the opens of the scheme `X`. -/
201	private abbrev opensTopology (X : Scheme.{u}) : GrothendieckTopology (TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :=
202	  Opens.grothendieckTopology (X : TopCat)
203	
204	open MorphismProperty in
205	/-- **Localization criterion for module sheafification.**  The scheme-level module
206	sheafification `sheafification.map f` is an isomorphism of sheaves of modules iff the
207	underlying abelian-presheaf morphism `(PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map f` lies in
208	the weak-equivalence class `J.W` of the opens topology (i.e. is a local isomorphism of
209	abelian-group presheaves).  This is the reduction step of
210	`isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`: it turns the strong-monoidality comparison
211	into a purely abelian local-isomorphism statement.  Project-local: it specialises
212	`_root_.PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms` to the
213	identity morphism of `X.ringCatSheaf.obj`. -/
214	lemma isIso_sheafification_map_iff {P Q : X.PresheafOfModules} (f : P ⟶ Q) :
215	    IsIso (sheafification.map f) ↔
216	      (opensTopology X).W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map f) := by
217	  have e := _root_.PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms
218	      (J := opensTopology X) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)
219	  constructor
220	  · intro h
221	    have h' : ((MorphismProperty.isomorphisms (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)).inverseImage
222	        (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj))) f := h
223	    rw [← e] at h'
224	    exact h'
225	  · intro h
226	    have h' : (((opensTopology X).W).inverseImage
227	        (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj)) f := h
228	    rw [e] at h'
229	    exact h'
230	
231	/-- **The sheafification unit is an abelian local isomorphism.**  The underlying
232	abelian-presheaf morphism of the module sheafification unit `η_P : P ⟶ P^#` is
233	*definitionally* the abelian sheafification unit `toSheafify J P.presheaf`
234	(`PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app`), which lies
235	in the weak-equivalence class `J.W` of the opens topology by
236	`GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify`.  Project-local: this is the `η_P ∈ J.W`
237	ingredient of the abelian-`J.W`-monoidality transfer underlying
238	`isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`. -/
239	lemma localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit (P : X.PresheafOfModules) :
240	    (opensTopology X).W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map
241	      ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app P)) := by
242	  rw [PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
243	  exact (opensTopology X).W_toSheafify _
244	
245	/-- **Sheafification inverts the localization unit.**  `sheafification.map η_P` is an
246	isomorphism of sheaves of modules (the reflective-localization unit becomes invertible
247	after sheafifying).  Obtained by feeding `localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit` through the
248	localization criterion `isIso_sheafification_map_iff`.  Project-local: the `m = 0`
249	launching pad and the un-whiskered special case of
250	`isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`. -/
251	lemma isIso_sheafification_map_unit (P : X.PresheafOfModules) :
252	    IsIso (sheafification.map
253	      ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app P)) :=
254	  (isIso_sheafification_map_iff _).mpr (localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit P)

966	Nitsure §4 inductive step). For `A` a noetherian domain and `d ≥ 0`, a finite
967	module `N` over the polynomial ring `P_d := A[X_1,…,X_d]`, there exist `m ∈ ℕ`

969	`T := N ⧸ range φ` is a torsion `P_d`-module. Equivalently, a short exact
970	sequence `0 → P_d^{⊕m} → N → T → 0` with `T` torsion.
971	
972	`m` is the generic rank: `dim_{Frac P_d} (Frac P_d ⊗_{P_d} N)`. The map `φ`
973	is the `P_d`-linear combination of `m` lifts `v : Fin m → N` whose images form a
974	`Frac P_d`-basis of the localisation `N_Q := LocalizedModule (P_d)⁰ N`. This step
975	is built over `P_d` directly — no inversion of any `g ∈ A` is required. -/
976	theorem gf_generic_rank_ses
977	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
978	    (d : ℕ) (N : Type*) [AddCommGroup N]
979	    [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N]
980	    [Module A N] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] :
981	    ∃ (m : ℕ) (φ : (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)
982	        →ₗ[MvPolynomial (Fin d) A] N),
983	      Function.Injective φ ∧
984	      Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) := by
985	  classical
986	  -- Notation: `P = A[X_1,…,X_d]`, its non-zero divisors `S`, fraction field `K`,
987	  -- localisation `NK = N_Q`, and the localisation map `ℓ : N → NK`.
988	  let P := MvPolynomial (Fin d) A
989	  let S := nonZeroDivisors P
990	  let K := Localization S
991	  let NK := LocalizedModule S N
992	  let ℓ : N →ₗ[P] NK := LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap S N
993	  -- `NK` is a finite-dimensional `K`-vector space; take a basis indexed by `Fin m`.
994	  let m := Module.finrank K NK
995	  let b : Module.Basis (Fin m) K NK := Module.finBasis K NK
996	  -- Lift each basis vector along `ℓ`, clearing its denominator.
997	  obtain ⟨lift, hlift⟩ :
998	      ∃ lift : Fin m → N × S, ∀ i, ((lift i).2 : P) • b i = ℓ (lift i).1 := by
999	    refine ⟨fun i => (IsLocalizedModule.surj S ℓ (b i)).choose, fun i => ?_⟩
1000	    exact (IsLocalizedModule.surj S ℓ (b i)).choose_spec
1001	  let v : Fin m → N := fun i => (lift i).1
1002	  -- The denominators map to units of `K`.
1003	  let u : Fin m → Kˣ := fun i => (IsLocalization.map_units K (lift i).2).unit
1004	  have hlv : ∀ i, ℓ (v i) = (↑(u i) : K) • b i := by
1005	    intro i
1006	    have hu : (↑(u i) : K) = algebraMap P K ((lift i).2 : P) :=
1007	      (IsLocalization.map_units K (lift i).2).unit_spec
1008	    rw [hu, algebraMap_smul]
1009	    exact (hlift i).symm
1010	  -- The images `ℓ (v i)` are linearly independent over `K` (unit multiples of a basis).
1011	  have hLIK : LinearIndependent K (fun i => ℓ (v i)) := by
1012	    have h := b.linearIndependent.units_smul u
1013	    have heq : (fun i => ℓ (v i)) = (u • (⇑b)) := by
1014	      funext i; rw [hlv i]; rfl
1015	    rw [heq]; exact h
1016	  -- Restrict scalars to `P` (the inclusion `P → K` is injective), then descend to `v`.
1017	  have hLIP : LinearIndependent P (fun i => ℓ (v i)) := by
1018	    refine hLIK.restrict_scalars ?_
1019	    intro x y hxy
1020	    apply IsFractionRing.injective P K
1021	    rw [Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one, Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one]
1022	    exact hxy
1023	  have hLIv : LinearIndependent P v := LinearIndependent.of_comp ℓ hLIP
1024	  -- The map `φ : P^{⊕m} → N`, `e_i ↦ v i`.
1025	  refine ⟨m, Fintype.linearCombination P v, ?_, ?_⟩
1026	  · -- Injectivity of `φ` is exactly linear independence of `v`.
1027	    rw [← LinearMap.ker_eq_bot, LinearMap.ker_eq_bot']
1028	    intro g hg
1029	    rw [Fintype.linearCombination_apply] at hg
1030	    funext i
1031	    exact Fintype.linearIndependent_iff.mp hLIv g hg i
1032	  · -- The cokernel `T = N ⧸ range φ` is torsion: every element is killed by a
1033	    -- non-zero divisor of `P`.
1034	    intro x
1035	    obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := Submodule.Quotient.mk_surjective _ x
1036	    -- The images `ℓ (v i)` span `NK` over `K` (they are unit multiples of a basis).
1037	    have hspan : Submodule.span K (Set.range (fun i => ℓ (v i))) = ⊤ := by
1038	      refine top_unique ?_
1039	      rw [← b.span_eq, Submodule.span_le]
1040	      rintro _ ⟨i, rfl⟩
1041	      have hbi : b i = (↑(u i) : K)⁻¹ • ℓ (v i) := by
1042	        rw [hlv i, smul_smul, inv_mul_cancel₀ (u i).ne_zero, one_smul]
1043	      rw [hbi]
1044	      exact Submodule.smul_mem _ _ (Submodule.subset_span ⟨i, rfl⟩)
1045	    -- Hence `ℓ n` is a `K`-linear combination of the `ℓ (v i)`.
1046	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := (Submodule.mem_span_range_iff_exists_fun K).mp
1047	      (show ℓ n ∈ Submodule.span K (Set.range fun i => ℓ (v i)) by
1048	        rw [hspan]; exact Submodule.mem_top)
1049	    -- Clear the denominators of the finitely many coefficients `c i`.
1050	    obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := IsLocalization.exist_integer_multiples S Finset.univ c
1051	    have ha : ∀ i, ∃ y : P, algebraMap P K y = (s : P) • c i := fun i =>
1052	      hs i (Finset.mem_univ i)
1053	    choose a hae using ha
1054	    -- After multiplying by `s`, `ℓ (s • n)` becomes `ℓ (φ a)`.
1055	    have key : ℓ ((s : P) • n) = ℓ (Fintype.linearCombination P v a) := by
1056	      rw [map_smul, ← hc, Fintype.linearCombination_apply, map_sum, Finset.smul_sum]
1057	      refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
1058	      rw [map_smul]
1059	      -- `(s:P) • (c i • ℓ v_i) = a i • ℓ v_i`
1060	      rw [← algebraMap_smul K ((s : P)) (c i • ℓ (v i)), smul_smul, ← Algebra.smul_def,
1061	        ← hae i, algebraMap_smul]
1062	    -- So `s • n - φ a` lies in the kernel of `ℓ`, hence is killed by some `t ∈ S`.
1063	    have hker : ℓ ((s : P) • n - Fintype.linearCombination P v a) = 0 := by
1064	      rw [map_sub, key, sub_self]
1065	    obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := (IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff S ℓ).mp hker
1066	    -- The non-zero divisor `t * s` annihilates the class of `n` in `T`.
1067	    refine ⟨t * s, ?_⟩
1068	    rw [smul_sub, sub_eq_zero] at ht
1069	    have ht2 : (↑t : P) • ((↑s : P) • n) = (↑t : P) • Fintype.linearCombination P v a := ht
1070	    have hmem : (↑(t * s) : P) • n ∈ LinearMap.range (Fintype.linearCombination P v) := by
1071	      rw [Submonoid.coe_mul, mul_smul, ht2, ← map_smul]
1072	      exact LinearMap.mem_range_self _ _
1073	    -- Conclude `(t * s) • ⟦n⟧ = 0`.
1074	    rw [← Submodule.Quotient.mk_smul, Submodule.Quotient.mk_eq_zero]
1075	    exact hmem
1076	
1077	/-- **L5b.1 — annihilator extraction for the torsion module**
1078	(`lem:gf_torsion_annihilator`, Nitsure §4). For `A` a noetherian domain, `d ≥ 0`,
1079	and `T` a finite *torsion* module over `P_d := A[X_1,…,X_d]`, there exists a
1080	non-zero `F ∈ Ann_{P_d}(T)`. Since `P_d` is a domain, a non-zero-divisor
1081	annihilator (provided by `Submodule.annihilator_top_inter_nonZeroDivisors`) is the
1082	same thing as a non-zero one. -/
1083	theorem gf_torsion_annihilator
1084	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A] (d : ℕ)
1085	    (T : Type*) [AddCommGroup T] [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
1086	    [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
1087	    (htors : Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T) :
1088	    ∃ F : MvPolynomial (Fin d) A, F ≠ 0 ∧
1089	      F ∈ Module.annihilator (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T := by
1090	  obtain ⟨F, hFann, hFnzd⟩ := Submodule.annihilator_top_inter_nonZeroDivisors htors
1091	  refine ⟨F, nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero hFnzd, ?_⟩
1092	  rw [← Submodule.annihilator_top]
1093	  exact hFann
1094	
1095	/-! ### L5b.2 — Nagata change of variables (`lem:gf_nagata_monic_lastVar`)
1096	
1097	The single-variable elimination of the support-dimension drop needs a triangular
1098	change of variables `e` making a non-zero `F ∈ A[X_0,…,X_n]` monic in `X_0` up to a
1099	unit after inverting one `g ∈ A`. Mathlib's Noether-normalisation development
1100	(`Mathlib.RingTheory.NoetherNormalization`) constructs exactly this transformation
1101	`T` and proves the leading coefficient is a unit — but only over a *field*, and as
1102	`private` lemmas. The block below replays that construction over a noetherian *domain*
1103	(the field is used in Mathlib only to conclude the top coefficient is a unit; over a
1104	domain it is merely non-zero, and becomes a unit after inverting it). The degree
1105	bookkeeping (`degreeOf_zero_t`, `leadingCoeff_finSuccEquiv_t`, `T_leadingcoeff_eq`) is
1106	a domain-adapted transcription of the Mathlib originals. -/
1107	
1108	section NagataNormalization
1109	
1110	-- `AlgebraicGeometry.Polynomial` exists and would shadow the root namespace, so the
1111	-- `Polynomial`/`MvPolynomial` lemma APIs are opened with explicit `_root_.` prefixes.
1112	open _root_.Polynomial _root_.MvPolynomial _root_.Ideal Nat _root_.RingHom List
1113	
1114	/-- `finSuccEquiv` commutes with coefficient base change `MvPolynomial.map phi`. -/
1115	private theorem finSuccEquiv_map_comm {A B : Type*} [CommRing A] [CommRing B]
1116	    (phi : A →+* B) (m : ℕ) (q : MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A) :
1117	    finSuccEquiv B m (MvPolynomial.map phi q)
1118	      = Polynomial.map (MvPolynomial.map phi) (finSuccEquiv A m q) := by
1119	  induction q using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
1120	  | C a => simp [finSuccEquiv_apply]
1121	  | add p q hp hq => simp [hp, hq]
1122	  | mul_X p i hp =>
1123	    simp only [map_mul, Polynomial.map_mul, hp]
1124	    congr 1
1125	    cases i using Fin.cases with
1126	    | zero => simp [finSuccEquiv_X_zero]
1127	    | succ j => simp [finSuccEquiv_X_succ]
1128	
1129	variable {k : Type*} [CommRing k] [IsDomain k] {n : ℕ} (f : MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) k)
1130	variable (v w : Fin (n + 1) →₀ ℕ)
1131	
1132	local notation3 "up" => 2 + f.totalDegree
1133	local notation3 "r" => fun (i : Fin (n + 1)) ↦ up ^ i.1
1134	
1135	omit [IsDomain k] in
1136	variable {f v} in
1137	private lemma lt_up (vlt : ∀ i, v i < up) : ∀ l ∈ ofFn v, l < up := by grind
1138	
1139	/-- The triangular Nagata substitution `X_i ↦ X_i + c • X_0 ^ (up ^ i)` (`i ≠ 0`),
1140	`X_0 ↦ X_0`. Transcribed from `Mathlib.RingTheory.NoetherNormalization` to a domain. -/
1141	private noncomputable abbrev T1 (c : k) :
1142	    MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) k →ₐ[k] MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) k :=
1143	  aeval fun i ↦ if i = 0 then X 0 else X i + c • X 0 ^ r i
1144	
1145	omit [IsDomain k] in
1146	private lemma t1_comp_t1_neg (c : k) : (T1 f c).comp (T1 f (-c)) = AlgHom.id _ _ := by
1147	  rw [comp_aeval, ← MvPolynomial.aeval_X_left]; ext i v; cases i using Fin.cases <;> simp
1148	
1149	/-- The Nagata transformation as an `A`-algebra automorphism. -/
1150	private noncomputable abbrev T := AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom (T1 f 1) (T1 f (-1))
1151	  (t1_comp_t1_neg f 1) (by simpa using t1_comp_t1_neg f (-1))
1152	
1153	omit [IsDomain k] in
1154	private lemma sum_r_mul_ne (vlt : ∀ i, v i < up) (wlt : ∀ i, w i < up) (ne : v ≠ w) :
1155	    ∑ x : Fin (n + 1), r x * v x ≠ ∑ x : Fin (n + 1), r x * w x := by
1156	  intro h
1157	  refine ne <| Finsupp.ext <| congrFun <| ofFn_inj.mp ?_
1158	  apply ofDigits_inj_of_len_eq (Nat.lt_add_right f.totalDegree one_lt_two)
1159	    (by simp) (lt_up vlt) (lt_up wlt)
1160	  simpa only [ofDigits_eq_sum_mapIdx, mapIdx_eq_ofFn, get_ofFn, length_ofFn,
1161	    Fin.val_cast, mul_comm, sum_ofFn] using h
1162	
1163	private lemma degreeOf_zero_t {a : k} (ha : a ≠ 0) : ((T f) (monomial v a)).degreeOf 0 =
1164	    ∑ i : Fin (n + 1), (r i) * v i := by
1165	  rw [← natDegree_finSuccEquiv, monomial_eq, Finsupp.prod_pow v fun a ↦ X a]
1166	  simp only [Fin.prod_univ_succ, Fin.sum_univ_succ, map_mul, map_prod, map_pow,
1167	    AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom_apply, MvPolynomial.aeval_C, MvPolynomial.aeval_X, if_pos, Fin.succ_ne_zero,
1168	    ite_false, one_smul, map_add, finSuccEquiv_X_zero, finSuccEquiv_X_succ, algebraMap_eq]
1169	  have h (i : Fin n) :
1170	      (Polynomial.C (X (R := k) i) + Polynomial.X ^ r i.succ) ^ v i.succ ≠ 0 :=
1171	    pow_ne_zero (v i.succ) (leadingCoeff_ne_zero.mp <| by simp [add_comm, leadingCoeff_X_pow_add_C])
1172	  rw [natDegree_mul (by simp [ha]) (mul_ne_zero (by simp) (Finset.prod_ne_zero_iff.mpr
1173	    (fun i _ ↦ h i))), natDegree_mul (by simp) (Finset.prod_ne_zero_iff.mpr (fun i _ ↦ h i)),
1174	    natDegree_prod _ _ (fun i _ ↦ h i), natDegree_finSuccEquiv, degreeOf_C]
1175	  simpa only [natDegree_pow, zero_add, natDegree_X, mul_one, Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_mul,
1176	    add_right_inj] using Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ ↦ by
1177	    rw [add_comm (Polynomial.C _), natDegree_X_pow_add_C, mul_comm])
1178	
1179	private lemma degreeOf_t_ne_of_ne (hv : v ∈ f.support) (hw : w ∈ f.support) (ne : v ≠ w) :
1180	    (T f <| monomial v <| coeff v f).degreeOf 0 ≠
1181	    (T f <| monomial w <| coeff w f).degreeOf 0 := by
1182	  rw [degreeOf_zero_t _ _ <| mem_support_iff.mp hv, degreeOf_zero_t _ _ <| mem_support_iff.mp hw]
1183	  refine sum_r_mul_ne f v w (fun i ↦ ?_) (fun i ↦ ?_) ne <;>
1184	  exact lt_of_le_of_lt ((monomial_le_degreeOf i ‹_›).trans (degreeOf_le_totalDegree f i)) (by lia)
1185	
1186	private lemma leadingCoeff_finSuccEquiv_t :
1187	    (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) ((monomial v) (coeff v f)))).leadingCoeff =
1188	    algebraMap k _ (coeff v f) := by
1189	  rw [monomial_eq, Finsupp.prod_fintype]
1190	  · simp only [map_mul, map_prod, leadingCoeff_mul, leadingCoeff_prod]
1191	    rw [AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom_apply, algHom_C, algebraMap_eq, finSuccEquiv_apply, eval₂Hom_C, coe_comp]
1192	    simp only [AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom_apply, Function.comp_apply, leadingCoeff_C, map_pow,
1193	      leadingCoeff_pow, algebraMap_eq]
1194	    have : ∀ j, ((finSuccEquiv k n) ((T1 f) 1 (X j))).leadingCoeff = 1 := fun j ↦ by
1195	      by_cases h : j = 0
1196	      · simp [h, finSuccEquiv_apply]
1197	      · simp only [aeval_eq_bind₁, bind₁_X_right, if_neg h, one_smul, map_add, map_pow]
1198	        obtain ⟨i, rfl⟩ := Fin.exists_succ_eq.mpr h
1199	        simp [finSuccEquiv_X_succ, finSuccEquiv_X_zero, add_comm]
1200	    simp only [this, one_pow, Finset.prod_const_one, mul_one]
1201	  exact fun i ↦ pow_zero _
1202	
1203	/-- Over a domain, the `T`-transform of a non-zero `f` has, in `X_0`, a leading
1204	coefficient equal to `C (coeff v f)` for some `v ∈ f.support` (hence non-zero). The
1205	Mathlib original concludes `IsUnit` using the field hypothesis; here we keep the exact
1206	coefficient so the caller can invert it. -/
1207	private lemma T_leadingcoeff_eq (fne : f ≠ 0) :
1208	    ∃ v ∈ f.support,
1209	      (finSuccEquiv k n (T f f)).leadingCoeff = MvPolynomial.C (coeff v f) := by
1210	  obtain ⟨v, vin, vs⟩ := Finset.exists_max_image f.support
1211	    (fun v ↦ (T f ((monomial v) (coeff v f))).degreeOf 0) (support_nonempty.mpr fne)
1212	  set h := fun w ↦ (MvPolynomial.monomial w) (coeff w f)
1213	  simp only [← natDegree_finSuccEquiv] at vs
1214	  replace vs : ∀ x ∈ f.support \ {v}, (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h x))).degree <
1215	      (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v))).degree := by
1216	    intro x hx
1217	    obtain ⟨h1, h2⟩ := Finset.mem_sdiff.mp hx
1218	    apply degree_lt_degree <| lt_of_le_of_ne (vs x h1) ?_
1219	    simpa only [natDegree_finSuccEquiv]
1220	      using degreeOf_t_ne_of_ne f _ _ h1 vin <| ne_of_not_mem_cons h2
1221	  have coeff : (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v + ∑ x ∈ f.support \ {v}, h x))).leadingCoeff =
1222	      (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v))).leadingCoeff := by
1223	    simp only [map_add, map_sum]
1224	    rw [add_comm]
1225	    apply leadingCoeff_add_of_degree_lt <| (lt_of_le_of_lt <| degree_sum_le _ _) ?_
1226	    have h2 : h v ≠ 0 := by simpa [h] using mem_support_iff.mp vin
1227	    replace h2 : (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v))) ≠ 0 := fun eq ↦ h2 <|
1228	      by simpa only [map_eq_zero_iff _ (AlgEquiv.injective _)] using eq
1229	    exact (Finset.sup_lt_iff <| Ne.bot_lt (fun x ↦ h2 <| degree_eq_bot.mp x)).mpr vs
1230	  refine ⟨v, vin, ?_⟩
1231	  rw [leadingCoeff_finSuccEquiv_t] at coeff
1232	  nth_rw 2 [← f.support_sum_monomial_coeff]
1233	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_add_sum_diff_singleton_of_mem vin h, coeff, algebraMap_eq]
1234	
1235	/-- **L5b.2 — Nagata change of variables: monic in the distinguished variable**
1236	(`lem:gf_nagata_monic_lastVar`, Nitsure §4). For `A` a domain and `0 ≠ F ∈
1237	A[X_0,…,X_m]`, there is a triangular `A`-algebra automorphism `e` and a non-zero
1238	`g ∈ A` such that, after inverting `g`, the image of `e F` in `A_g[X_0,…,X_m]` —
1239	viewed via `finSuccEquiv` as a univariate polynomial in `X_0` — has a *unit* leading
1240	coefficient. (Variable convention: `finSuccEquiv` singles out `X_0`, not `X_m`; the
1241	choice is immaterial up to renaming.) -/
1242	theorem gf_nagata_monic_lastVar
1243	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] (m : ℕ)
1244	    (F : MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A) (hF : F ≠ 0) :
1245	    ∃ (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0)
1246	      (e : MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A ≃ₐ[A] MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A),
1247	      IsUnit
1248	        (MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv (Localization.Away g) m
1249	          (MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g)) (e F))).leadingCoeff := by
1250	  obtain ⟨v, vin, hlc⟩ := T_leadingcoeff_eq F hF
1251	  have hg0 : coeff v F ≠ 0 := mem_support_iff.mp vin
1252	  refine ⟨coeff v F, hg0, T F, ?_⟩
1253	  set g := coeff v F with hg
1254	  haveI : Nontrivial (Localization.Away g) := by
1255	    have hle : Submonoid.powers g ≤ nonZeroDivisors A := by
1256	      rw [Submonoid.powers_le]; exact mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_ne_zero hg0
1257	    exact (IsLocalization.injective (Localization.Away g) hle).nontrivial
1258	  have hgunit : IsUnit (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) g) :=
1259	    IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit g
1260	  have hCunit : IsUnit (MvPolynomial.C (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g) g)
1261	      : MvPolynomial (Fin m) (Localization.Away g)) := hgunit.map MvPolynomial.C
1262	  have hne : (MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g)))
1263	      (finSuccEquiv A m (T F F)).leadingCoeff ≠ 0 := by
1264	    rw [hlc, MvPolynomial.map_C]; exact hCunit.ne_zero
1265	  rw [finSuccEquiv_map_comm, leadingCoeff_map_of_leadingCoeff_ne_zero _ hne, hlc,
1266	    MvPolynomial.map_C]
1267	  exact hCunit
1268	
1269	end NagataNormalization
1270	
1271	/-- Compatibility of `finSuccEquiv` with the constant-variable inclusion
1272	`rename Fin.succ`: the composite `S = R[X_1,…,X_n] → R[X_0,…,X_n] → S[X_0]` is the
1273	coefficient inclusion `Polynomial.C`. Project-local because Mathlib only records the
1274	`optionEquivLeft` variant (`finSuccEquiv_rename_finSuccEquiv`). -/
1275	private theorem finSuccEquiv_rename_succ
1276	    (R : Type*) [CommRing R] (n : ℕ) (s : MvPolynomial (Fin n) R) :
1277	    MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv R n (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.succ s) = Polynomial.C s := by
1278	  induction s using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
1279	  | C r => rw [MvPolynomial.rename_C, MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv_apply, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]; rfl

1310	    (hp.unit⁻¹).isUnit

1354	/-- Pull back an `R`-module structure along an additive equivalence `e : M ≃+ N`, defining
1355	`r • y := e (r • e.symm y)`. With this structure `e` is `R`-linear; used in the torsion
1356	reindex to transport the reindexed module structure from the `P`-localisation of the torsion
1357	module to the goal's `A`-localisation. -/
1358	@[reducible] def pullbackModuleAddEquiv {R M N : Type*} [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid M]
1359	    [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R M] (e : M ≃+ N) : Module R N where
1360	  smul r y := e (r • e.symm y)
1361	  one_smul y := by change e (1 • e.symm y) = y; rw [one_smul, AddEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
1362	  mul_smul r s y := by
1363	    change e ((r * s) • e.symm y) = e (r • e.symm (e (s • e.symm y)))
1364	    rw [AddEquiv.symm_apply_apply, mul_smul]
1365	  smul_zero r := by change e (r • e.symm 0) = 0; rw [map_zero, smul_zero, map_zero]
1366	  smul_add r x y := by
1367	    change e (r • e.symm (x + y)) = e (r • e.symm x) + e (r • e.symm y)
1368	    rw [map_add, smul_add, map_add]
1369	  add_smul r s y := by
1370	    change e ((r + s) • e.symm y) = e (r • e.symm y) + e (s • e.symm y)
1371	    rw [add_smul, map_add]
1372	  zero_smul y := by change e (0 • e.symm y) = 0; rw [zero_smul, map_zero]
1373	
1374	/-- Module-finiteness transports across the pulled-back structure of `pullbackModuleAddEquiv`:
1375	if `M` is a finite `R`-module then so is `N` under the pulled-back action. -/
1376	theorem finite_of_pullbackModuleAddEquiv {R M N : Type*} [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid M]
1377	    [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M] (e : M ≃+ N) :
1378	    @Module.Finite R N _ _ (pullbackModuleAddEquiv e) := by
1379	  letI : Module R N := pullbackModuleAddEquiv e
1380	  refine Module.Finite.equiv (M := M) (N := N)
1381	    { toFun := e, map_add' := map_add e, invFun := e.symm,
1382	      left_inv := e.left_inv, right_inv := e.right_inv,
1383	      map_smul' := fun r x => ?_ }
1384	  simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
1385	  change e (r • x) = e (r • e.symm (e x))
1386	  rw [AddEquiv.symm_apply_apply]
1387

1391	theorem pullback_isScalarTower {Ag R M N : Type*} [CommSemiring Ag] [Semiring R] [Algebra Ag R]
1392	    [AddCommMonoid M] [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R M] [Module Ag M] [IsScalarTower Ag R M]
1393	    (e : M ≃+ N) :
1394	    letI := pullbackModuleAddEquiv (R := R) e
1395	    letI := pullbackModuleAddEquiv (R := Ag) e
1396	    IsScalarTower Ag R N := by
1397	  letI := pullbackModuleAddEquiv (R := R) e
1398	  letI := pullbackModuleAddEquiv (R := Ag) e
1399	  refine ⟨fun a b x => ?_⟩
1400	  change e ((a • b) • e.symm x) = e (a • e.symm (e (b • e.symm x)))
1401	  rw [AddEquiv.symm_apply_apply, smul_assoc]

1408	theorem finite_of_quotientRingEquiv
1409	    {R B₁ B₂ M : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing B₁] [CommRing B₂] [AddCommGroup M]
1410	    [Algebra R B₁] [Algebra R B₂] [Module B₁ M] [Module R M] [IsScalarTower R B₁ M]
1411	    (ψ : B₁ ≃+* B₂)
1412	    (hψ : ∀ r : R, ψ (algebraMap R B₁ r) = algebraMap R B₂ r)
1413	    (hB₂ : Module.Finite R B₂) (hM : Module.Finite B₁ M) :
1414	    Module.Finite R M := by
1415	  haveI := hB₂
1416	  haveI := hM
1417	  have hsymm : ∀ r : R, ψ.symm (algebraMap R B₂ r) = algebraMap R B₁ r := by
1418	    intro r; rw [← hψ r, ψ.symm_apply_apply]
1419	  haveI : Module.Finite R B₁ :=
1420	    Module.Finite.equiv (AlgEquiv.ofRingEquiv (f := ψ.symm) hsymm).toLinearEquiv

1427	    {R' R M M' : Type*} [CommRing R'] [CommRing R] [Algebra R' R]
1428	    [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup M'] [Module R' M] [Module R M] [Module R' M'] [Module R M']
1429	    [IsScalarTower R' R M] [IsScalarTower R' R M']
1430	    (S : Submonoid R') (f : M →ₗ[R] M')
1431	    [IsLocalizedModule (S.map (algebraMap R' R)) f] :
1432	    IsLocalizedModule S (f.restrictScalars R') := by
1433	  apply IsLocalizedModule.mk
1434	  · intro s
1435	    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
1436	    have hmem : algebraMap R' R (s : R') ∈ S.map (algebraMap R' R) := ⟨s, s.2, rfl⟩
1437	    have hu := IsLocalizedModule.map_units f ⟨algebraMap R' R (s : R'), hmem⟩

1442	      rw [Module.algebraMap_end_apply, Module.algebraMap_end_apply, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]

1459	        ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul R (s : R') x₂, hs]

1838	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial
1839	    (A : Type u) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
1840	    (d : ℕ) (N : Type u) [AddCommGroup N]
1841	    [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N]

1843	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧

1845	  -- The genuine proof is a *strong* induction on `d`, with the module `N` and its
1846	  -- instances universally quantified in the motive: the inductive hypothesis must
1847	  -- apply to the lower-support-dimension torsion quotient `T` (a module over a
1848	  -- polynomial ring in fewer variables `m < d`), and the module instances depend
1849	  -- on `d`, so a faithful induction must re-quantify `N` over each `d`. The
1850	  -- `generalizing N` reverts `N` together with all five of its `d`-dependent
1851	  -- instances, so the IH has the shape
1852	  --   IH : ∀ m < d, ∀ (N : Type _) [AddCommGroup N] [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N]
1853	  --          [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N] [Module A N]
1854	  --          [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N],
1855	  --          ∃ f ≠ 0, Module.Free (A_f) (N_f).
1856	  induction d using Nat.strong_induction_on generalizing A N with
1857	  | _ d IH =>
1858	    rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos d with hd | hd
1859	    · -- Base case `d = 0`: `MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A ≅ A`, so `N` is module-finite
1860	      -- over `A` itself (via `Module.Finite.trans` through the iso), and the claim
1861	      -- is the finite-module leaf `exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`.
1862	      subst hd
1863	      haveI : Module.Finite A (MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A) :=
1864	        Module.Finite.equiv (MvPolynomial.isEmptyAlgEquiv A (Fin 0)).symm.toLinearEquiv
1865	      haveI : Module.Finite A N := Module.Finite.trans (MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A) N
1866	      exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A N
1867	    · -- Inductive step. Pass to `K = Frac A` and split on whether `N_K = 0`.
1868	      by_cases htors : Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) N)
1869	      · -- Torsion sub-case `N_K = 0`: closed by the L1 torsion base case applied
1870	        -- with the finite-type polynomial ring `B := A[X_1,…,X_d]` as the module's
1871	        -- ring of definition.
1872	        exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N htors
1873	      · -- Generic-rank dévissage (the genuine generic-rank residue): with `N_K ≠ 0`,
1874	        -- let `m` be the generic rank of `N` over the domain `A[X_1,…,X_d]`; choosing
1875	        -- `m` elements whose images form a `K(X)`-basis and clearing denominators
1876	        -- gives, after inverting some `g ≠ 0`, a short exact sequence
1877	        --   `0 → A_g[X]^{⊕m} → N_g → T → 0`
1878	        -- with `T` torsion of support dimension `< d`. After a Noether-normalisation
1879	        -- reindex `T` is finite over a polynomial ring in `m' < d` variables, so the
1880	        -- IH applies:  `IH m' (by …) T` yields `h ≠ 0` with `T_h` free over `A_h`.
1881	        -- The free middle term `A_g[X]^{⊕m}` localises to a free `A_{gh}`-module by
1882	        -- the `d = 0` leaf (`exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`) applied
1883	        -- coordinatewise, and `exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact` (L3)
1884	        -- splices the localised SES to give `N_f` free over `A_f` with `f := g·h`.
1885	        --
1886	        -- The IH is now genuinely in scope and quantifies over the base domain `A`
1887	        -- (the structural fix of this iter): `IH m' (hm' : m' < d) A_g T_g` typechecks
1888	        -- at the reindexed base `A_g = Localization.Away g`.
1889	        --
1890	        -- The generic-rank SES `0 → (Fin m → P) → N → T → 0` (`gf_generic_rank_ses`),
1891	        -- with `T := N ⧸ range φ` torsion over `P := A[X_1,…,X_d]`.
1892	        obtain ⟨m, φ, hφinj, hTtors⟩ := gf_generic_rank_ses A d N
1893	        -- Step 1: the torsion cokernel `N ⧸ range φ` is a finite `P`-module (its restricted
1894	        -- `A`-module structure + scalar tower `A → P → quotient` are found by instances).
1895	        haveI hTfin : Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) :=
1896	          Module.Finite.of_surjective (LinearMap.range φ).mkQ (LinearMap.range φ).mkQ_surjective
1897	        -- Step 2: reindex the torsion cokernel onto `m' < d` variables over `A_g`.
1898	        -- `gf_torsion_reindex` now emits its `MvPolynomial (Fin m') A_g`-action (`hmod1`) and the
1899	        -- scalar tower (`htower`) over the *canonical* (`inferInstance`) `Module A_g`-action on the
1900	        -- localised module — the redundant `Module A_g T_g` existential was dropped, so the
1901	        -- `A_g`-action the IH and `free_localizationAway_of_away_tower` synthesise is exactly the
1902	        -- one `htower` refers to.  This dissolves the former `OreLocalization`
1903	        -- instance-presentation diamond: there is now a single `Module A_g T_g` instance in play.
1904	        obtain ⟨g, hg0, m', hm'lt, hmod1, htower, hfin⟩ :=
1905	          gf_torsion_reindex A d hd (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) hTtors
1906	        -- Step 3: the inductive hypothesis at the new noetherian-domain base `A_g`.
1907	        haveI hdomg : IsDomain (Localization.Away g) :=
1908	          IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
1909	            (Submonoid.powers_le.mpr (mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_ne_zero hg0))
1910	        haveI hnoethg : IsNoetherianRing (Localization.Away g) := inferInstance
1911	        -- Register the reindex's `MvPolynomial (Fin m') A_g`-module structure, its finiteness, and
1912	        -- its scalar tower in the local instance cache so the IH application infers them.
1913	        letI := hmod1
1914	        haveI := hfin
1915	        haveI := htower
1916	        -- Steps 3–4: the IH at base `A_g` produces `hh ≠ 0` in `A_g` with the doubly-localised
1917	        -- `(T_g)_hh` free over `(A_g)_hh`; the tower-descent helper
1918	        -- `free_localizationAway_of_away_tower` descends that witness to a single `f := g·a ∈ A`.
1919	        obtain ⟨f, hf0, hTf_free⟩ :
1920	            ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧ Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
1921	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ)) := by
1922	          obtain ⟨hh, hh0, hfree_Tgh⟩ :=
1923	            IH m' hm'lt (Localization.Away g)
1924	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ))
1925	          exact free_localizationAway_of_away_tower A (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) hg0 hh0 hfree_Tgh
1926	        -- Step 5: splice the localised SES.  The free left end `Fin m → P` (a finite power
1927	        -- of the `A`-free polynomial ring `P`) localises to a free `A_f`-module by base
1928	        -- change, and `exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact` (L3) splices.
1929	        haveI hM'free_base : Module.Free A (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) := inferInstance
1930	        have hM'_free : Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
1931	            (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)) :=
1932	          (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers f) (Localization.Away f)
1933	            (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f)
1934	              (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A))).free
1935	        exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)
1936	          (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) φ
1937	          (LinearMap.range φ).mkQ hφinj (LinearMap.range φ).mkQ_surjective
1938	          (by rw [LinearMap.exact_iff, Submodule.ker_mkQ]) hf0 hf0 hM'_free hTf_free
1939	
1940	end GenericFreeness

1942	/-! ## Generic flatness, algebraic form (Nitsure §4 "Lemma on Generic Flatness")
1943	
1944	For a noetherian domain `A`, a finite-type `A`-algebra `B`, and a finite
1945	`B`-module `M` (viewed as an `A`-module through the scalar tower `A → B → M`),
1946	there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that `M_f` is free over `A_f`.
1947	
1948	The proof splits along the blueprint's decomposition:
1949	
1950	* **Primary route** (provided here, axiom-clean): when `M` is already
1951	  *module-finite over `A`*, this is the finite-module helper
1952	  `GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite` — over `Frac A` the
1953	  localised module is a finite vector space hence free, and
1954	  `Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers` descends that
1955	  freeness to a single basic open `D(f)`.
1956	
1957	* **Surviving residue** (`sorry` this iter): when `M` is finite over the
1958	  *finite-type* algebra `B` but not module-finite over `A`, the genuine §4
1959	  dévissage is required — a prime filtration of `M` as a finite `B`-module
1960	  reduces to `M = B/𝔭`, Noether normalisation makes `B_g` finite over the
1961	  polynomial ring `A_g[b₁,…,b_n]`, and induction on the support dimension
1962	  bottoms out at the polynomial-ring core of generic freeness. That core
1963	  (a finite module over `A[X₁,…,X_d]` is generically free) is the precise
1964	  piece Mathlib does not yet supply. -/
1965	
1966	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
1967	-- The §4 dévissage assembly in the `B/𝔭` branch (Noether normalisation, the
1968	-- polynomial-ring core, and the ring↔module localisation bridge) drives the deep
1969	-- localisation/quotient instance stack past the default elaboration and
1970	-- instance-search heartbeat limits; both are raised for this declaration.
1971	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 400000 in
1972	/-- **Generic flatness, algebraic form** (Nitsure §4 "Lemma on Generic
1973	Flatness"). Let `A` be a noetherian domain, `B` a finite-type `A`-algebra, and
1974	`M` a finite `B`-module regarded as an `A`-module via the scalar tower
1975	`A → B → M`. Then there exists `f ∈ A`, `f ≠ 0`, such that
1976	`LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M` is free over `Localization.Away f`.
1977	
1978	Blueprint: `thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`. The finite-`A`-module case is the
1979	thin Mathlib wrapper `GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`;
1980	the finite-type residue is the classical §4 dévissage (Noether normalisation +
1981	the polynomial-ring core, both proved above). -/
1982	theorem genericFlatnessAlgebraic
1983	    (A B M : Type u) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
1984	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B] [Algebra.FiniteType A B]
1985	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module.Finite B M]
1986	    [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M] :
1987	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
1988	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
1989	  by_cases hAM : Module.Finite A M
1990	  · -- Primary route: `M` module-finite over `A` ⟹ the finite-module helper.
1991	    haveI := hAM
1992	    exact GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A M
1993	  · -- Surviving residue: `M` finite over the finite-type algebra `B` but not
1994	    -- module-finite over `A`. Dévissage over the noetherian ring `B`
1995	    -- (`IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime`) with motive
1996	    -- `P N := ∃ f ≠ 0, Free A_f (N_f)`, each `B`-module `N` carrying its restricted
1997	    -- `A`-action through `algebraMap A B`. The subsingleton obligation discharges
1998	    -- from the torsion base case (L1); the short-exact obligation from the L3
1999	    -- splice; the `N ≅ B/𝔭` obligation is the genuine residual (it needs L4
2000	    -- finiteness + L5, the polynomial-ring core still under construction).
2001	    haveI : IsNoetherianRing B := Algebra.FiniteType.isNoetherianRing A B
2002	    have key : letI : Module A M := Module.compHom M (algebraMap A B)
2003	        ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
2004	          Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
2005	      refine IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime B
2006	        (inferInstance : Module.Finite B M)
2007	        (motive := fun N _ _ _ => letI : Module A N := Module.compHom N (algebraMap A B)
2008	          ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
2009	            Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) N))
2010	        ?_ ?_ ?_
2011	      · -- subsingleton `N` ⟹ torsion base case (L1).
2012	        intro N _ _ _ _
2013	        letI : Module A N := Module.compHom N (algebraMap A B)
2014	        haveI : IsScalarTower A B N := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
2015	        refine GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion A B N ?_
2016	        exact LocalizedModule.subsingleton_iff.mpr fun m =>
2017	          ⟨1, Submonoid.one_mem _, by rw [Subsingleton.elim m 0, smul_zero]⟩
2018	      · -- `N ≅ B/𝔭` (domain quotient, finite-type over `A`): the genuine residual.
2019	        -- L4 (`exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial`) and L5
2020	        -- (`exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial`) are now BOTH closed and
2021	        -- axiom-clean, so the remaining work here is pure assembly:
2022	        --   1. Set `C := B ⧸ p.asIdeal` (a finite-type `A`-algebra DOMAIN, via
2023	        --      `Ideal.Quotient.isDomain` + `Algebra.FiniteType.of_surjective`), and
2024	        --      transport the goal from `N` to `C` along the `B`-linear (hence
2025	        --      `A`-linear) equiv `a`, since freeness through `LocalizedModule` is
2026	        --      preserved by an `A`-linear equivalence.
2027	        --   2. Split on `Subsingleton (C ⊗_A K)` (`K = Frac A`).  If torsion, close
2028	        --      with the L1 leaf `exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion A B N`.
2029	        --   3. Otherwise apply L4 to get `g ≠ 0`, `n`, and an injective `A_g`-algebra
2030	        --      map `φ : A_g[X_n] → C_g` with `C_g := Localization.Away (algebraMap A C g)`
2031	        --      module-finite over `A_g[X_n]` (via `φ.toAlgebra`).  Feed `C_g` (as a
2032	        --      finite `MvPolynomial (Fin n) A_g`-module) to L5 at base `A_g`, obtaining
2033	        --      `h ≠ 0` in `A_g` with `(C_g)_h` free over `(A_g)_h`.
2034	        --   4. Bridge the ring-localisation `C_g = Localization.Away (algebraMap A C g)`
2035	        --      to the module-localisation `LocalizedModule (powers g) C` (an
2036	        --      `IsLocalizedModule`-uniqueness iso), then descend the `A_g`-witness `h`
2037	        --      to a single `f ∈ A` via `free_localizationAway_of_away_tower`.
2038	        -- The only genuinely fiddly step is the ring↔module localisation bridge in (4);
2039	        -- everything else is direct application of the now-closed L1/L4/L5 leaves.
2040	        intro N _ _ _ p a
2041	        letI : Module A N := Module.compHom N (algebraMap A B)
2042	        haveI : IsScalarTower A B N := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
2043	        classical
2044	        -- Step 1. Pass to the domain quotient `C := B ⧸ p.asIdeal`, a finite-type
2045	        -- `A`-algebra domain. `a` transports the goal between `N` and `C`.
2046	        haveI hp : p.asIdeal.IsPrime := p.isPrime
2047	        haveI : IsDomain (B ⧸ p.asIdeal) := Ideal.Quotient.isDomain p.asIdeal
2048	        haveI : Algebra.FiniteType A (B ⧸ p.asIdeal) :=
2049	          Algebra.FiniteType.of_surjective
2050	            (Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ A p.asIdeal) (Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ_surjective A p.asIdeal)
2051	        suffices hC : ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧ Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
2052	            (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (B ⧸ p.asIdeal)) by
2053	          obtain ⟨f, hf, hCfree⟩ := hC
2054	          refine ⟨f, hf, ?_⟩
2055	          haveI := hCfree
2056	          -- `LocalizedModule (powers f) C ≃ₗ[A_f] LocalizedModule (powers f) N`,
2057	          -- the localisation functor applied to `a.symm`, transfers freeness.
2058	          exact Module.Free.of_equiv
2059	            (IsLocalizedModule.mapEquiv (Submonoid.powers f)
2060	              (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f) (B ⧸ p.asIdeal))
2061	              (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f) N)
2062	              (Localization.Away f) ((a.restrictScalars A).symm))
2063	        -- Step 2. Torsion / non-torsion split on `C_K = K ⊗_A C`.
2064	        by_cases htors :
2065	            Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) (B ⧸ p.asIdeal))
2066	        · -- Torsion: the L1 leaf with `B := M := C`.
2067	          exact GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion A
2068	            (B ⧸ p.asIdeal) (B ⧸ p.asIdeal) htors
2069	        · -- Step 3–4. Non-torsion: Noether normalisation (L4) + polynomial core
2070	          -- (L5) + the ring↔module bridge + descent (`away_tower`).
2071	          set C := B ⧸ p.asIdeal with hCdef
2072	          -- `C_K = K ⊗_A C` is nontrivial (the non-torsion hypothesis), via the
2073	          -- identification `LocalizedModule (A⁰) C ≃ K ⊗_A C`.
2074	          haveI : Nontrivial (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) C) :=
2075	            not_subsingleton_iff_nontrivial.mp htors
2076	          have hCK : Nontrivial (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) C) :=
2077	            (LocalizedModule.equivTensorProduct (nonZeroDivisors A) C).injective.nontrivial
2078	          -- Step 3. Noether normalisation: invert `g` so that `C_g` is module-finite
2079	          -- over `A_g[X_1,…,X_n]` through the injective `A_g`-algebra map `φ`.
2080	          obtain ⟨n, g, hg, algBg, φ, hφinj, hφfin, hcompat⟩ :=
2081	            GenericFreeness.exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial A C hCK
2082	          set Cg := Localization.Away (algebraMap A C g) with hCgdef
2083	          letI := algBg
2084	          letI := φ.toAlgebra
2085	          haveI : Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin n) (Localization.Away g)) Cg := hφfin
2086	          haveI : IsDomain (Localization.Away g) :=
2087	            IsLocalization.isDomain_of_le_nonZeroDivisors _
2088	              (Submonoid.powers_le.mpr (mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_ne_zero hg))
2089	          haveI : IsNoetherianRing (Localization.Away g) :=
2090	            IsLocalization.isNoetherianRing (Submonoid.powers g) _ inferInstance
2091	          haveI : IsScalarTower (Localization.Away g)
2092	              (MvPolynomial (Fin n) (Localization.Away g)) Cg :=
2093	            IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun r => (φ.commutes r).symm
2094	          -- Polynomial core (L5) at base `A_g`: invert `h ∈ A_g` to free `C_g`.
2095	          obtain ⟨h, hh, hCgfree⟩ :=
2096	            GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial (Localization.Away g) n Cg
2097	          -- Step 4. Bridge `LocalizedModule (powers g) C ≃ₗ[A_g] C_g`. The ambient
2098	          -- `A → C → C_g` tower exhibits `C_g` as the localisation of the `A`-module
2099	          -- `C` at `powers g` (instances inferred); `hcompat` aligns the `A_g`-action
2100	          -- with it, upgrading the `A`-linear bridge to an `A_g`-linear one.
2101	          haveI : IsScalarTower A (Localization.Away g) Cg :=
2102	            IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun x => (hcompat x).symm
2103	          let e0 : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) C ≃ₗ[A] Cg :=
2104	            IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers g)
2105	              (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom A C Cg).toLinearMap
2106	          let e : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) C ≃ₗ[Localization.Away g] Cg :=
2107	            LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (Submonoid.powers g)
2108	              (Localization.Away g) e0
2109	          -- (v) lift through `LocalizedModule (powers h) (·)` and transfer L5 freeness.
2110	          have hfree' : Module.Free (Localization.Away h)
2111	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h)
2112	                (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) C)) := by
2113	            haveI := hCgfree
2114	            exact Module.Free.of_equiv
2115	              (IsLocalizedModule.mapEquiv (Submonoid.powers h)
2116	                (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers h) Cg)
2117	                (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers h)
2118	                  (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) C))
2119	                (Localization.Away h) e.symm)
2120	          -- Descend the `A_g`-witness to a single `f ∈ A`.
2121	          exact GenericFreeness.free_localizationAway_of_away_tower A C hg hh hfree'
2122	      · -- short-exact closure ⟹ the L3 splice.
2123	        intro N₁ _ _ _ N₂ _ _ _ N₃ _ _ _ i q hi hq hex hN₁ hN₃
2124	        letI : Module A N₁ := Module.compHom N₁ (algebraMap A B)
2125	        letI : Module A N₂ := Module.compHom N₂ (algebraMap A B)
2126	        letI : Module A N₃ := Module.compHom N₃ (algebraMap A B)
2127	        haveI : IsScalarTower A B N₁ := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
2128	        haveI : IsScalarTower A B N₂ := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
2129	        haveI : IsScalarTower A B N₃ := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl

2132	        exact GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact A B N₁ N₂ N₃
2133	          i q hi hq hex hf' hf'' hF' hF''
2134	    -- Transport the dévissage conclusion (restricted `A`-action via `algebraMap A B`)
2135	    -- to the ambient `A`-module structure on `M`; they agree by the scalar tower.
2136	    have hAinst : (Module.compHom M (algebraMap A B) : Module A M) = ‹Module A M› := by
2137	      refine Module.ext_iff.mpr ?_
2138	      funext a m
2139	      change algebraMap A B a • m = a • m
2140	      rw [Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one, smul_assoc, one_smul]

2144	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — G1 affine-local finiteness (Stacks 01PB)
2145	
2146	The geometric form of generic flatness (`genericFlatness`) consumes the
2147	affine-local finiteness of the section module of a finite-type quasi-coherent
2148	sheaf (blueprint G1, `lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections`, Stacks Tag 01PB).
2149	This section builds the **locality reduction** for that statement: given a finite
2150	basic-open cover of an affine open `W` on each member of which the section module
2151	is module-finite, the section module on `W` is module-finite. It is assembled from
2152	the QUOT keystone `Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen` (gap2: the section
2153	restriction `Γ(F,W) → Γ(F, D(g))` is `IsLocalizedModule (powers g)`) together with
2154	Mathlib's finite-generation gluing `Module.Finite.of_localizationSpan_finite`.
2155	
2156	The remaining (genuinely geometric, Mathlib-absent) ingredient toward a complete
2157	G1 is the *base case*: on an affine where the finite-type generating sections of
2158	`F` are global, the section module is finite — i.e. the sheaf epimorphism
2159	`free I → F|_{D(g)}` (with `I` finite) is module-level surjective on global affine
2160	sections. That bridge (sheaf-epi ⟹ stalkwise surjective ⟹ module generation) is
2161	flagged in the file `task_results` handoff. -/
2162	
2163	/-- **Transfer of module finiteness across localized-module models.** If `Rₚ`
2164	localizes `R` at `S` and `φ : M →ₗ[R] N` exhibits `N` as the localization of `M`

2171	Mathlib has the canonical-model finiteness instance (needing global finiteness) but

2173	theorem finite_localizedModule_of_isLocalizedModule
2174	    {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (S : Submonoid R) {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
2175	    {Rₚ : Type*} [CommRing Rₚ] [Algebra R Rₚ] [IsLocalization S Rₚ]
2176	    {N : Type*} [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [Module Rₚ N] [IsScalarTower R Rₚ N]
2177	    (φ : M →ₗ[R] N) [IsLocalizedModule S φ] [Module.Finite Rₚ N] :
2178	    Module.Finite (Localization S) (LocalizedModule S M) := by
2179	  classical
2180	  let e : N ≃ₗ[R] LocalizedModule S M :=
2181	    IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv S φ (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap S M)
2182	  let ψ : Rₚ ≃ₐ[R] Localization S := IsLocalization.algEquiv S Rₚ (Localization S)
2183	  have hsemi : ∀ (a : Rₚ) (x : N), e (a • x) = ψ a • e x := by
2184	    intro a x
2185	    obtain ⟨⟨r, s⟩, hrs⟩ := IsLocalization.mk'_surjective S a
2186	    have hsa : (s : R) • a = (algebraMap R Rₚ) r := by
2187	      rw [Algebra.smul_def, ← hrs, mul_comm, IsLocalization.mk'_spec]
2188	    have hunit : IsUnit ((algebraMap R (Module.End R (LocalizedModule S M))) (s : R)) :=
2189	      IsLocalizedModule.map_units (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap S M) s
2190	    have hbij := (Module.End.isUnit_iff _).mp hunit
2191	    apply hbij.injective
2192	    change (algebraMap R (Module.End R (LocalizedModule S M)) (s : R)) (e (a • x))
2193	       = (algebraMap R (Module.End R (LocalizedModule S M)) (s : R)) (ψ a • e x)
2194	    simp only [Module.algebraMap_end_apply]
2195	    rw [← map_smul e ((s:R)) (a • x)]
2196	    have hL : (s:R) • (a • x) = (r:R) • x := by
2197	      rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul Rₚ (s:R) (a • x), smul_smul, ← Algebra.smul_def, hsa,
2198	        IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]
2199	    have hR : (s:R) • (ψ a • e x) = (r:R) • e x := by
2200	      rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul (Localization S) (s:R) (ψ a • e x), smul_smul,
2201	        ← Algebra.smul_def, ← map_smul ψ, hsa, ψ.commutes, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]
2202	    rw [hL, map_smul e, hR]
2203	  obtain ⟨T, hT⟩ := (Module.Finite.fg_top (R := Rₚ) (M := N))
2204	  refine ⟨⟨T.image (e : N → LocalizedModule S M), ?_⟩⟩
2205	  rw [Finset.coe_image, eq_top_iff]
2206	  suffices h : ∀ z ∈ Submodule.span Rₚ (T : Set N),
2207	      e z ∈ Submodule.span (Localization S) (e '' (T : Set N)) by
2208	    intro y _
2209	    have := h (e.symm y) (by rw [hT]; trivial)
2210	    rwa [e.apply_symm_apply] at this
2211	  intro z hz
2212	  induction hz using Submodule.span_induction with
2213	  | mem z hz => exact Submodule.subset_span ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩
2214	  | zero => simp
2215	  | add a b _ _ ha hb => rw [map_add]; exact Submodule.add_mem _ ha hb
2216	  | smul a b _ hb => rw [hsemi]; exact Submodule.smul_mem _ _ hb

2218	/-- **G1 locality reduction (Stacks 01PB, geometric half).** Let `F` be a
2219	quasi-coherent sheaf of modules on a scheme `X` and `W` an affine open. If a finite
2220	family `t ⊆ Γ(X, W)` generates the unit ideal (so the basic opens `D(g)`, `g ∈ t`,
2221	cover `W`) and on each `D(g)` the section module `Γ(F, D(g))` is finite over
2222	`Γ(X, D(g))`, then the section module `Γ(F, W)` is finite over `Γ(X, W)`.
2223	
2224	Assembles the gap2 keystone `Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen` (section
2225	restriction to `D(g)` is `IsLocalizedModule (powers g)`) — with `Γ(X, D(g))` the
2226	away-localization `IsLocalization.Away g` (`IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_basicOpen`)
2227	— transported to the canonical model via `finite_localizedModule_of_isLocalizedModule`,
2228	and concluded by `Module.Finite.of_localizationSpan_finite`. Project-local: this is
2229	the locality glue of blueprint G1; the missing piece toward a full G1 is the
2230	finite-type base case (finitely many generating sections ⟹ module finite). -/
2231	theorem gf_finite_sections_of_basicOpen_finite_cover
2232	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
2233	    {W : X.Opens} (hW : IsAffineOpen W)
2234	    (t : Finset Γ(X, W)) (ht : Ideal.span (t : Set Γ(X, W)) = ⊤)
2235	    (H : ∀ g ∈ t, Module.Finite Γ(X, X.basicOpen g) Γ(F, X.basicOpen g)) :
2236	    Module.Finite Γ(X, W) Γ(F, W) := by
2237	  apply Module.Finite.of_localizationSpan_finite t ht
2238	  intro g
2239	  letI : Module Γ(X, W) Γ(F, X.basicOpen g.val) :=
2240	    Module.compHom _ (algebraMap Γ(X, W) Γ(X, X.basicOpen g.val))
2241	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(X, W) Γ(X, X.basicOpen g.val) Γ(F, X.basicOpen g.val) :=
2242	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)
2243	  haveI : IsLocalization.Away (g.val) Γ(X, X.basicOpen g.val) :=
2244	    hW.isLocalization_basicOpen g.val
2245	  haveI := Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen F hW g.val
2246	  haveI : Module.Finite Γ(X, X.basicOpen g.val) Γ(F, X.basicOpen g.val) := H g.val g.2
2247	  exact finite_localizedModule_of_isLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g.val)

2250	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — G1 base case via the affine tilde–Γ adjunction
2251	
2252	The base case of blueprint G1 (`lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections`, Stacks 01PB):
2253	on an affine `V ≅ Spec R`, a quasi-coherent sheaf globally generated by finitely
2254	many sections has a finite section module. The crux is **seam 2**
2255	(`gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi`): the affine global-sections functor `moduleSpecΓFunctor`
2256	sends a sheaf epimorphism of quasi-coherent modules to a *surjective* `R`-module
2257	map. This is the structural content of "H¹(affine, qcoh) = 0" delivered for free by
2258	Mathlib's affine `tilde.adjunction` counit (`fromTildeΓNatTrans`) — no cohomology
2259	build. Recipe: `analogies/gf-gamma-exact.md`. -/
2260	
2261	/-- **Seam 2 — `Γ` on affine quasi-coherent modules sends epis to surjections.**
2262	For `G, F : (Spec R).Modules` with both tilde–Γ counits `IsIso` (the quasi-coherent
2263	hypothesis, supplied by `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict` /
2264	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`) and `π : G ⟶ F` an epimorphism of sheaves of
2265	modules, the affine global-sections map `(moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π).hom` is a
2266	surjective `R`-linear map.
2267	
2268	Mechanism (no cohomology): the counit `fromTildeΓNatTrans` is natural in `π`, so
2269	`~(Γπ) = G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π ≫ inv F.fromTildeΓ` — a composite of an iso, the epi `π`,
2270	and an iso, hence an epi. `tilde.functor R` is faithful, so it reflects epimorphisms;
2271	therefore `Γπ` is epi in `ModuleCat R`, i.e. surjective. Project-local: Mathlib's
2272	`tilde.adjunction` supplies every ingredient but not this packaged corollary. -/
2273	theorem gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {G F : (Spec R).Modules}
2274	    (π : G ⟶ F) [Epi π] [IsIso G.fromTildeΓ] [IsIso F.fromTildeΓ] :
2275	    Function.Surjective (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π).hom := by
2276	  rw [← ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective]
2277	  refine (tilde.functor R).epi_of_epi_map ?_

2279	  simp only [Functor.comp_map, Functor.id_map] at hnat
2280	  change (tilde.functor R).map (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π) ≫ F.fromTildeΓ
2281	       = G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π at hnat
2282	  have h : (tilde.functor R).map (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π)
2283	         = (G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π) ≫ inv F.fromTildeΓ :=
2284	    (IsIso.eq_comp_inv F.fromTildeΓ).mpr hnat
2285	  rw [h]

2287	  exact epi_comp (G.fromTildeΓ ≫ π) (inv F.fromTildeΓ)

2301	    [IsIso G.fromTildeΓ] [IsIso F.fromTildeΓ]
2302	    [Module.Finite R (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj G)] :
2303	    Module.Finite R (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F) :=
2304	  Module.Finite.of_surjective (moduleSpecΓFunctor.map π).hom (gf_affine_qcoh_Gamma_epi π)

2320	    (π : (tilde.functor R).obj N ⟶ F) [Epi π] :
2321	    Module.Finite R (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F) := by
2322	  haveI hiso : IsIso ((tilde.functor R).obj N).fromTildeΓ :=
2323	    (inferInstance : IsIso ((tilde.adjunction (R := R)).counit.app ((tilde.functor R).obj N)))

2330	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — GF seam 1 (Stacks 01PB, standard finite cover)
2331	
2332	The seam-1 declarations of blueprint Half 2 (Stacks Tag 01PB): from the abstract
2333	finite-type local-generation datum of a quasi-coherent sheaf, extract a *standard*
2334	finite basic-open cover of an affine open `W` on each member of which the sheaf is
2335	globally generated by finitely many sections (equivalently, admits a finite free
2336	epimorphism). These feed the G1 locality reduction `gf_finite_sections_of_basicOpen_finite_cover`
2337	together with the affine base case `gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_free_epi`.
2338	
2339	* 1b `gf_affine_finite_standard_subcover` — pure affine-scheme topology: an arbitrary
2340	  open cover of an affine open `W` refines to a finite standard basic-open subcover
2341	  `{D(g) : g ∈ t}` with `Ideal.span t = ⊤`, each `D(g)` contained in a cover member.

2343	  as a finite-index free epimorphism `free I ↠ M`. -/
2344	
2345	/-- **Seam 1b — finite standard basic-open subcover of an affine open.** Given an
2346	affine open `W` of a scheme `X` and an arbitrary open cover `{U i}` of `W`
2347	(`W ≤ ⨆ i, U i`), there is a finite family `t ⊆ Γ(X, W)` with
2348	`Ideal.span t = ⊤` (so the basic opens `D(g)`, `g ∈ t`, cover `W`) such that each
2349	`D(g)` is contained in some cover member `U i`. Mirrors the affine-communication
2350	template `AlgebraicGeometry.of_affine_open_cover`: each point of `W` sits in a basic
2351	open `D(f) ≤ U i` by `IsAffineOpen.exists_basicOpen_le`, the basic opens span by
2352	`IsAffineOpen.self_le_iSup_basicOpen_iff`, and quasi-compactness (packaged as
2353	`Ideal.span_eq_top_iff_finite`) extracts a finite subfamily. Project-local: the
2354	refinement-with-containment is not packaged in Mathlib. -/
2355	theorem gf_affine_finite_standard_subcover
2356	    {X : Scheme.{u}} {W : X.Opens} (hW : IsAffineOpen W)
2357	    {ι : Type*} (U : ι → X.Opens) (hcov : W ≤ ⨆ i, U i) :
2358	    ∃ t : Finset Γ(X, W), Ideal.span (t : Set Γ(X, W)) = ⊤ ∧
2359	      ∀ g ∈ t, ∃ i, X.basicOpen g ≤ U i := by
2360	  classical
2361	  -- For each point of `W`, pick a basic open `D(f) ≤ U i` containing it.
2362	  have hpt : ∀ x : W, ∃ f : Γ(X, W), (∃ i, X.basicOpen f ≤ U i) ∧ (x : X) ∈ X.basicOpen f := by
2363	    intro x
2364	    obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp (hcov x.2)
2365	    obtain ⟨f, hf_le, hf_mem⟩ := hW.exists_basicOpen_le (V := U i) ⟨x.1, hi⟩ x.2
2366	    exact ⟨f, ⟨i, hf_le⟩, hf_mem⟩
2367	  choose f hf_cover hf_mem using hpt
2368	  -- The basic opens `D(f x)` cover `W`, so the `f x` span `Γ(X, W)`.
2369	  have hspan : Ideal.span (Set.range f) = ⊤ := by
2370	    rw [← hW.self_le_iSup_basicOpen_iff]
2371	    intro x hx
2372	    rw [iSup_range', TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup]
2373	    exact ⟨_, hf_mem ⟨x, hx⟩⟩
2374	  obtain ⟨t, ht_sub, ht_span⟩ := (Ideal.span_eq_top_iff_finite _).mp hspan
2375	  refine ⟨t, ht_span, ?_⟩
2376	  intro g hg
2377	  obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := ht_sub hg
2378	  exact hf_cover x

2390	theorem gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi.{v', u'} {C : Type u'} [Category.{v'} C]
2391	    {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
2392	    [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
2393	    [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
2394	    (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) :
2395	    (∃ σ : M.GeneratingSections, σ.IsFiniteType) ↔
2396	      (∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (π : SheafOfModules.free I ⟶ M), Epi π) := by
2397	  constructor
2398	  · rintro ⟨σ, hσ⟩

2400	  · rintro ⟨I, hI, π, hπ⟩

2405	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — transport of generating sections along a functor
2406	
2407	`GeneratingSections.map` is the `GeneratingSections` analogue of Mathlib's
2408	`SheafOfModules.Presentation.mapGenerators`/`Presentation.map`. Given a
2409	colimit-preserving functor `F : SheafOfModules R ⥤ SheafOfModules S` together with a
2410	unit-iso `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S`, a (finite) generating family of `M` transports to a
2411	(finite) generating family of `F.obj M` with the *same* index type. This is the engine of
2412	seam 1a (`gf_localGenerators_restrict`): restriction of a sheaf of modules along an open
2413	immersion is such a functor (via the project's slice-to-geometric equivalence
2414	`overRestrictEquiv`, whose unit-iso is `overRestrictUnitIso`), so finite generation
2415	survives restriction. Project-local: Mathlib packages this transport only for full
2416	`Presentation`s (kernel data included), not for bare generating families. -/
2417	
2418	namespace SheafOfModules.GeneratingSections
2419	
2420	universe v₁ u₁ v₂ u₂
2421	
2422	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
2423	  [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
2424	  [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
2425	  {C' : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} C'] {J' : GrothendieckTopology C'} {S : Sheaf J' RingCat.{u}}
2426	  [HasSheafify J' AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [J'.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
2427	  [J'.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
2428	
2429	/-- Transport a generating family of `M` along a colimit-preserving functor `F` carrying
2430	the unit to the unit (`η : F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S`): the images
2431	`(mapFree F η σ.I).inv ≫ F.map σ.π` of the generators generate `F.obj M`, with the index
2432	type unchanged.
2433	
2434	The colimit-preservation hypothesis `hF` is taken as an *explicit* argument (rather than an
2435	instance) so that callers supply it directly: instance search for
2436	`PreservesColimitsOfSize` on the `def`-backed `Scheme.Modules` functors (e.g.
2437	`Scheme.Modules.pullback`) does not reliably fire through the `Modules` type abbreviation. -/
2438	noncomputable def map {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (σ : M.GeneratingSections)
2439	    (F : SheafOfModules.{u} R ⥤ SheafOfModules.{u} S)
2440	    (η : F.obj (SheafOfModules.unit R) ≅ SheafOfModules.unit S)
2441	    (hF : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u} F) :
2442	    (F.obj M).GeneratingSections where
2443	  I := σ.I
2444	  s := (F.obj M).freeHomEquiv ((letI := hF; SheafOfModules.mapFree F η σ.I).inv ≫ F.map σ.π)
2445	  epi := by
2446	    letI := hF
2447	    letI : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{0, 0} F := preservesColimitsOfSize_shrink _
2448	    rw [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
2449	    haveI : Epi (F.map σ.π) := inferInstance
2450	    exact epi_comp _ _

2453	@[simp] theorem map_I {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (σ : M.GeneratingSections)
2454	    (F : SheafOfModules.{u} R ⥤ SheafOfModules.{u} S)
2455	    (η : F.obj (SheafOfModules.unit R) ≅ SheafOfModules.unit S)
2456	    (hF : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u} F) :
2457	    (map σ F η hF).I = σ.I := rfl

2460	unchanged). Stated as a theorem with explicit `hF` since `map` takes its colimit-preservation
2461	witness explicitly. -/
2462	theorem map_isFiniteType {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (σ : M.GeneratingSections) [σ.IsFiniteType]

2684	/-! ## Generic flatness (Nitsure §4)
2685	
2686	Over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type
2687	`X ⟶ S` is flat above some non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. This is the inductive
2688	engine of the flattening-stratification theorem: combined with
2689	Noetherian induction on the closed complement `S ∖ V`, it produces the
2690	finite stratification of `S` by flatness loci.
2691	
2692	Algebraically (theorem `generic_flatness_algebraic`, no Lean pin): for a
2693	noetherian domain `A`, a finite-type `A`-algebra `B`, and a finite
2694	`B`-module `M`, there exists a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that `M_f` is a
2695	free `A_f`-module. The geometric form (this declaration) restricts to a
2696	non-empty affine open `Spec A ⊆ S` and applies the algebraic form on
2697	each finite-type-algebra patch of `X` above `Spec A`.
2698	
2699	Blueprint reference: `thm:generic_flatness` (Nitsure §4). -/
